summary: Corrected the declared and call-site argument list for mpLib_8004F008 by adding a missing integer argument before the float coordinate/range parameters. The fix updates the mplib.h prototype from eight integer-like parameters before the floats to nine, then inserts an explicit 0 at known call sites in ftCo_800A0FB0, mpColl_80043C6C, and mpIsland_8005AC14. In ftCo_800A0FB0 this also allowed removal of an unused volatile stack temporary that had been used for matching around the incorrect call shape.
what_changed: Changed the mpLib_8004F008 declaration in src/melee/mp/mplib.h to take one additional int before its five trailing float parameters.
Updated ftCo_800A0FB0 to call mpLib_8004F008 with two consecutive zero integer arguments before arg7 through arg11.
Removed a volatile int sp8 temporary and its assignment in ftCo_800A0FB0 after the corrected function signature made the extra stack-shaping variable unnecessary.
Updated mpColl_80043C6C to pass an additional 0 before temp_f1_3, temp_f2, sp20, sp24, and 0.0f.
Updated mpIsland_8005AC14 to pass an additional 0 before the Vec3-derived float arguments.
smart_moves: Fixed the root cause in the shared header prototype rather than only adjusting individual callers.
Propagated the corrected signature to every changed call site shown in the PR, keeping the header and callers synchronized.
Used explicit 0 placeholders for the newly identified integer parameter, preserving existing behavior while aligning the call ABI.
Removed obsolete volatile stack manipulation in ftCo_800A0FB0 once the real argument list accounted for the needed call shape.
decomp_lessons: When a call to an unknown or partially understood function requires volatile stack padding to match, re-check the callee prototype and argument count before preserving artificial locals.

For functions with runs of integer parameters followed by float parameters, a missing integer can shift the interpreted boundary between GPR and FPR arguments and make multiple callers look subtly wrong.
Header corrections should be made together with call-site updates so future decompilation work sees the same ABI assumptions everywhere.
A zero literal inserted before the float arguments is a strong clue that the missing parameter belongs to the integer/control-flag group, not the coordinate float group.
naming_conventions: Retained existing autogenerated-style symbol name mpLib_8004F008 because the semantic name of the function was not established in this PR.
Kept nearby mpLib declarations in address-comment format such as /* 04F008 */ bool mpLib_8004F008(...).
No new semantic names for the newly added argument were introduced; the prototype still uses unnamed primitive parameters.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Correcting the prototype added a missing integer argument before float parameters, which likely better matches the PowerPC calling convention register allocation at call sites.
Eliminating volatile int sp8 in ftCo_800A0FB0 indicates the prior local was a matching workaround rather than meaningful program state.
The consistent insertion point across ftCommon, mpcoll, and mpisland call sites supports the revised ABI shape for mpLib_8004F008.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the text excerpt is empty, so no actionable reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find the definition of mpLib_8004F008 and confirm the role of the newly added integer parameter.
Search for any remaining mpLib_8004F008 call sites not included in this slice to verify all callers use the corrected signature.
Inspect whether related mpLib functions near 8004F008 have similar integer/float prototype boundary issues.
Review historical uses of volatile stack temporaries around unknown calls as candidates for prototype corrections.
